openFindings.title: WRITE prompt omits component facts while requiring exact component values
md-generator output guard does not enforce the documented spec-folder or sandbox boundary
Extracted live-site values enter the WRITE prompt without prompt-data isolation
Report/preview catalog promises overwrite protection the scripts do not implement
Guided run validates relative output against one cwd but executes extraction from another
openFindings.description: The guided wrapper accepts and preflights the documented repo-root relative output path, but extraction runs from BACKEND_ROOT with the original relative path, so extract.ts resolves the same argument inside the skill/backend tree and rejects it as unsafe.
openFindings.scopeProof: Focused reads of guided-run, extract, backend README, CLI comments, and guided-run tests show the relative path is accepted, planned unchanged, executed from BACKEND_ROOT, and only tested as a string plan.

openFindings.title: Dark-mode CSS variable values are injected into report style attributes without data isolation
Transition shorthand parsing splits cubic-bezier commas into bogus transitions
Report and preview renderers inject source-derived CSS token strings into style contexts without validation
openFindings.description: P1-006 generalizes beyond dark-mode swatches: report-gen.ts and preview-gen.ts interpolate source-derived typography, radius, and shadow token strings into inline CSS contexts without a CSS-value sanitizer or property-specific allowlist.
openFindings.scopeProof: Exact sink grep plus direct reads covered report-gen.ts, preview-gen.ts, proof.ts, token value types, Google Fonts link producer, and backend rendering tests.

openFindings.title: Procedure-card schema lint is manual-only and outside the existing canon checker
Byte-identical benchmark artifacts remain under two incompatible naming conventions
Focused extraction modules mostly have no real test files

searchDebt.rationale: Iteration 1 was explicitly broad inventory, not line-by-line dimension review.
searchDebt.reason: Review plan assigns deep dimension work to later iterations.

searchDebt.rationale: Glob confirmed broad playbook presence, but full scenario-to-capability validation is not needed to adjudicate the mode-routing and md-generator correctness focus.
searchDebt.reason: Defer to traceability or maintainability iterations for full scenario validation.

searchDebt.rationale: Carry forward to iteration 7 for residual backend coverage.
searchDebt.reason: Prompt constrained this pass to the six P1-remediation backend files; non-output extraction modules were inventoried by glob but not read line-by-line in this iteration.

searchDebt.rationale: code_graph_status reported stale readiness and graphless fallback was used.
searchDebt.reason: Code graph readiness is stale, so graph assertions were not trusted.

searchDebt.rationale: Code graph readiness is stale, so graph assertions were not trusted.
searchDebt.reason: code_graph_status reported stale readiness and graphless fallback was used.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: No missing parent/phase structure was confirmed during inventory.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: Directory and targeted glob evidence matched the declared structure.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: Unchecked hard blockers were not silently left as complete; the visible P0/P1 unchecked rows are part of the accepted Phase 012 descope record.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: Accepted descope and P2 deferral evidence explains the unchecked rows.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: No mode-count or tool-surface mismatch was observed.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: Direct registry read matched the expected five-mode taxonomy.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: Compiled backend output exists and includes the package main target.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: Direct directory read confirmed dist/cli.js exists.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: Search did not produce an obvious actionable stub residue finding within inventory scope.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: Observed hits were semantically expected uses of the searched terms.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: Registry, hub-router, hub prose, and five mode frontmatters agree on the five-mode taxonomy and tool-surface split.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: Direct reads matched the declared routing contract.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: No correctness failure was confirmed in the sampled CLI parse, output guard, fast default, or validator pass/fail logic.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: No confirmed contract failure in those sampled entrypoints.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: Phase 010 spec and summary consistently record the remediation file layout and validation results.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: No residual correctness drift found in sampled Phase 010 records.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: Phase 012 explicitly records accepted descope and unscored dimensions rather than fabricating the expanded benchmark completion.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: The descope is explicit and not a silent false-pass claim.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: The guided wrapper uses spawnSync with argv arrays and no shell option; user URL/output values are passed as arguments, not shell-interpolated command strings.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: No command-injection path was confirmed because execution uses argv arrays with default shell=false.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: The crawler normalizes URL inputs, keeps discovered links on the same hostname, and skips forms/submits/navigation/action-intent controls before clicking reveal UI.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: No crawler side-effect vulnerability was confirmed in the sampled URL/link/click path.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: Registry entries and command/mode frontmatters preserve Read/Glob/Grep-only boundaries for interface/foundations/motion/audit and reserve Write/Edit/Bash for md-generator.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: No read-only mode escalation was confirmed in registry, mode frontmatter, or command frontmatter.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: P1-001 does not currently add a component-facts prompt-injection path because component facts are missing rather than interpolated; however, its future remediation must apply data isolation.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: No separate current security finding for P1-001 was confirmed beyond the new broader prompt-data isolation finding.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: Phase 010 implementation summary and checklist match observed package layout and sampled catalog content.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: No residual false-completion gap found in sampled Phase 010 evidence.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: Spec, decision record, checklist, and implementation summary repeatedly mark unbuilt expanded-battery/live/advisor work as UNSCORED, NOT MET, or accepted descope.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: No fabricated score or silent pass row found in sampled Phase 012 reconciliation evidence.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: Sampled scenario files map to live SKILL Procedure Card Selection and fallback sections across interface, foundations, motion, audit, and md-generator.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: No sampled scenario claimed a non-existent procedure-card capability.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: Command frontmatters and load steps still map to the five registry modes and tool surfaces; Phase 013 states the refactor is planning-only.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: No command projection mismatch found in frontmatter or mode binding samples.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: The observed duplication is directory-level summary text, not parallel authoritative bodies likely to diverge on the next capability change.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: Root catalogs summarize capability areas and link leaf files rather than duplicating long-form source details.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: A future implementer can identify the unbuilt categories, harness fields, live-mode rerun, and baseline promotion without re-deriving context.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: ADR-003, plan/task/checklist reconciliation, and follow-up items repeatedly name the descoped expanded-battery work and the future pickup path.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: Prompt facts/data isolation belongs in the write-prompt seam; output boundary/overwrite semantics belong in a shared output/artifact policy used by all write scripts.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: No separate P1 is needed; the active P1s can be addressed through two shared remediation seams instead of unrelated patches.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: P1-002 plus P1-003 remain important, but their combination does not currently justify P0.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: No reviewed dataflow lets live-site prompt text control output path arguments or automatic arbitrary file writes; output paths are CLI-controlled before extraction and prompt generation.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: A remediation plan should use two shared seams rather than four unrelated point fixes or one overbroad utility.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: The active P1s split into prompt/facts encoding and output/artifact policy seams; no single validateOutputPath or sanitizePromptData helper fixes all four prior P1s.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: Writes remain fixed artifact paths under caller outputDir, with the existing missing overwrite guard as the confirmed defect.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: The broader pass reconfirmed P1-004 overwrite semantics but did not identify a separate report/preview/proof P1.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: The current test asserts captured:false/consistent:false on empty data and captured:true with a real focus style.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: The focus absence/presence regression has a real focused test and was not re-reported.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: The reviewed write-prompt facts use support/count/consistency summaries for these surfaces.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: No separate raw source-derived prompt/report P1 was confirmed for boundary/icon/motion beyond counts or structured values in this pass.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: Proof style colors are hardcoded mappings or generated from numeric RGB, and screenshot src values are base64 buffers.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: No source-derived arbitrary CSS string was found in proof style declarations.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: Reviewed href/src sinks did not prove attribute breakout: href is HTML-escaped and screenshot src values are produced as base64.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: No unescaped quote-breaking href/src sink was confirmed in the reviewed files; Google Fonts host allowlisting remains hardening, not this P1.

ruledOutCandidates.rationale: No new P2 was needed because P2-003 already records focused extraction/rendering test gaps; this iteration adds rendering-test evidence to that advisory.
ruledOutCandidates.reason: The absence of rendering tests supports P1-008 and existing P2-003, but does not require a new distinct P2.
